title: Budgets, Interest, Loans, and Financial Decisions · Quantity Clinic: Compound Interest
summary: Compare household plans, emergency savings, credit costs, and loan offers without hiding assumptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg compound interest increment in an explainable household decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model compound growth by period and distinguish nominal from effective change.
Calculate simple interest and distinguish principal, rate, and time units.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Compound Interest evidence
steps.instruction: $1300 grows once per year at 5.5% for 2 years. Calculate the compounded value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1300, r=0.055, and t=2.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1300, r=0.055, and t=2.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

steps.title: Defend the Compound Interest model

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
